title: Deep CPS continuation chains SIGILL (ch4 amb nondeterministic search, n>=~16)
goal: Found by the SICP gate (ch4_amb). Deep CPS continuation chains (amb/nondeterministic backtracking) SIGILL when the search depth exceeds ~16; the test is capped at n=13. Bucket: deep-CPS-continuation-depth — likely stack/continuation allocation growth or a depth limit in the closure/continuation codegen. Relates to ESH-0048 (multishot call/cc).
acceptance: deep amb search (n>=20) completes without SIGILL; -r AND AOT
campaign_payoff: Deepens SICP ch4 nondeterministic-programming support; general CPS/continuation robustness.
status_note: MERGED #93 / 7ac01da5b64cdd858af597ad7c6afde74b5f9ece. Root cause: native O0 skipped cleanup passes entirely, leaving very large continuation/closure frames for deep amb backtracking; LLDB showed crash around eshkol_check_recursion_depth after thousands of CPS/amb frames. Initial fix 47338adb runs a small native O0 function cleanup pipeline (sroa,early-cse,instcombine<no-verify-fixpoint>,simplifycfg) while preserving WASM default<O1>. Windows ARM lite then timed out compiling validation_01_type_detection.esk; follow-up 34b039e9 skips the native O0 cleanup pipeline only for aarch64-windows, reducing a focused object compile proxy from 164.41s to 0.21s while keeping WASM and non-Windows native cleanup behavior. Verification: JIT/AOT deep amb n=20 ALL PASS, continuation_edge_test 30/30, host validation_01_type_detection compile/run PASS, windows_suite_surface_test PASS, ICC ready/100, CI 14/14 SUCCESS.
